Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and marked proteinuria with abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying renal pathology and possible systemic disease.
